content.full_text:
Re:
Farn PourlcAI- PRACTTcES CouvussroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-56ffi . Fax (916) 322-0886
December 22,2004
Philip D. Kohn, CityAttorney
City of Laguna Beach
Rutan & Tucker, LLP
611 Anton Boulevard, Fourteenth Floor
Costa Mesa, CA 92626-1931
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A.-04-258
Dear Mr. Kohn:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of Elizabeth Pearson,
Councilmember for the City of Laguna Beach for advice regarding the statement of
economic interests and conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the'Act').r
QUESTTONS
1. What reporting, if any, on her staternent of economic interests must
Councilmember Pearson make relating to the salary income paid by Hunsaker &
Associates, Inc., to Mr. Schneider prior to their marriage?
2- Is CouncilmemberPearson subject to the disqualification requirement relating
to conflicts of interest in section 87100 by reason of the salary income puia Uy Hunsaker
& Associates, Inc. to Mr. Schneider prior to their marriage?
CONCLUSIONS
1. Councilmember Pemson is not required to report on her state,rnent of economic
interests salary income eamed and received by Mr. Schneider from Hunsaker &
Associates, Inc. before the date of their marriage.
I Government Code sections 8 1000 - gl0l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2,sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-04-258
Page No.2
2. Salary income eamed and received by Mr. Schneider from Hunsaker &
Associates, Inc. prior to his marriage to Councilmember Pearson, will not give rise to a
conflict of interest for her.
FACTS
Councilmember Pearson was swom into office in December of 2002. In October
of 2004, Ms. Pearson married Emie Schneider. From May of 1995 to August of 2004,
Mr. Schneider was a salaried employee of Hunsaker & Associates, Inc. ("Hunsaker,), a
corporation that performs planning engineerin& and surveying services. Mr. Schneider
performed business development functions for the company. At no time during his
employment did he serve as a corporate officer or director nor did he hold (nor does he
now hold) any ownership interest in the company. While Ernie Schneider was involved
from time-to-time in providing services to individual clients of the company, he never
received any compensation paid directly by such clients. Following the conclusion of his
employment with Hunsaker in August of 2004,he joined the land brokerage firm of
O'Donnell/Atkins.
Prior to the date of their mariage, Councilmember Pearson and Mr. Schneider did
not commingle assets and did not jointly purchase or own any assets or property. They
did not reside at the same address. They did not execute a pre-nuptial agreement of any
kind.
You state that prior to the marriage, Mr. Schneider's salary income paid by
Hunsaker was his separate property. There was and is no agreement between them, and
Councilmernber Pearson had no community property interest in the income received by
Mr. Schneider from Hunsaker prior to their marriage.
Clients of Hunsakermaybe involved in futureprojects located in the City of
Laguna Beach for which discretionary govemmental decisions may be sought. Also
Hunsaker may itself be involved with regard to such projects.
ANALYSIS
You ask whether salary income that a council member's spouse received prior to
their marriage gives rise to any reporting or disqualification obligations for the council
member.
With respect to reporting, the Act requires officials to disclose reportable
investnents, business positions, interests in real property and income on their staternents
of economic interests, filed annually and upon assuming and leaving office. (Section
87302(b).) An official is required to report on their statement of economic interests
(Form 700) their communityproperty interest (50W in their spouse's income. The
ofiEcial must report his or her spouse's employer's name and all other required
information.


File No. A-O4-258
Page No. 3
With respect to disqualification, the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure
that public officials "perform their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused
by their own financial interests or the financial interests ofpersons who have supported
them." (Section 81001(b).) Specifically, section 8Tl00prohibits any public official from
making, participating in making, or otherwise using his or her official position to
influence a govemmental decision in which the official has a financial interest.
An official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of section
87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official or a mernber of his
orher immediate family or on, among otherthings:
"(c) Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial
lending institution made in the regular course ofbusiness on ter:ns
available to the public without regard to official status, aggregating five
hundred dollars ($SOO; or more in value provided orpromised to, received
by, the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made." (Section 87103(c).)
Under the Act, the income of a public official includes any community property
interest in the income of a spouse. The Act defines "in@me" in section 82030, in
pertinent part, as follows:
"(a) 'Income' means, excqrt as provided in subdivision (b), a payment
received, including but not limited to any salary, wage, advance, dividend,
interest, re,nt, proceeds from any sale, gift, including any grft of food or
beverage, loan, forgiveness or payment of indebtedness received by the
filer, reimbursement for expenses, perdiem, or contribution to an
insurance or pension program paid by anyperson other than an employer,
and including any community propefi interest in the income of a qtouse."
(Emphasis added.)
Thus Councilmenrber Pearson will generally be considered to have an economic
interest in any significant source of income to her spouse, Mr. Schneider, and such a
sollrce of income is reportable and may grve rise to a conflict of interest for her. (See,
e.g.,In re Roberts (2004) 17 FPPC Ops. 9.) Councilmernber Pearson must report on her
statement of economic interests, the community property interest (50o/o) in salary income
that Mr. Schneider received after their marriage.
Under the Act's definition of income, however, Councilmember Pearson does not
have a community property interest in salary income received by her husband from
Hunsakerbeforetheirmarriage. (StoneAdviceLetter, No. I-01-014, stating"...an
individual has a community property interest in his or her spouse's salary which is equal
to half the amount of any salary eamed during marriage.")2 Councilmember Pearson is
2 See In re Wilson's Marriage (1974) ll2 Cal. Rptr. 405, 519 P -2d 165, 10 Cal. 3d 851 (California
Supreme Court held that only consideration eamed during the marriage was community property, and


File No. A-04-258
Page No. 4
not required to report salary income earned and received by Mr. Schneider before their
marriage on her statement of economic interests, nor will it give rise to a conflict of
interest for her.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
. Wagner
Counsel, Legal
HPwjg
I:\Adviceltrs\04-258
therefore, pension benefits eamed by a spouse partly before and partly after marriage should be apportioned
between separate and community property.)

QUESTION: 1. What reporting, if any, on her staternent of economic interests must
Councilmember Pearson make relating to the salary income paid by Hunsaker &
Associates, Inc., to Mr. Schneider prior to their marriage?
2- Is CouncilmemberPearson subject to the disqualification requirement relating
to conflicts of interest in section 87100 by reason of the salary income puia Uy Hunsaker
& Associates, Inc. to Mr. Schneider prior to their marriage?

CONCLUSION: 1. Councilmember Pemson is not required to report on her state,rnent of economic
interests salary income eamed and received by Mr. Schneider from Hunsaker &
Associates, Inc. before the date of their marriage.
I Government Code sections 8 1000 - gl0l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2,sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2. Salary income eamed and received by Mr. Schneider from Hunsaker &
Associates, Inc. prior to his marriage to Councilmember Pearson, will not give rise to a
conflict of interest for her.
